message:
fix(ssr): prevent browser crashes and hydration mismatches in Three.js Canvas components

Fixes critical SSR issues with React Three Fiber components that were causing
browser crashes with "Cannot read properties of null (reading 'addEventListener')"

Root cause:
- OrbitControls.connect() was attempting to attach event listeners during SSR
- No DOM canvas element exists on the server, causing null reference errors
- Original code removed SSR guard in commit 27ccad4 during optimization

Changes:
- Add proper SSR guards to all Canvas components using mounted state pattern
- Prevents hydration mismatch warnings by ensuring consistent server/client renders
- Follows React Rules of Hooks (all hooks called before conditional return)

Components fixed:
- CovarianceScene.tsx: Added useState + useEffect mount tracking
- BridgeViz.tsx: Added useState + useEffect mount tracking + useEffect import
- WingViz.tsx: Changed from typeof window check to mounted state
- TransformerViz.tsx: Changed from typeof window check to mounted state

Pattern used:
const [mounted, setMounted] = useState(false);
useEffect(() => { setMounted(true); }, []);
if (!mounted) return null;

This ensures:
✓ Server renders null (no OrbitControls instantiation)
✓ Client first render also returns null (matches server - no hydration mismatch)
✓ After mount, useEffect sets mounted=true, triggering re-render with Canvas
✓ No console warnings, no crashes, smooth hydration
